headline: Founder + CEO at KEVANI
summary:
Kevin Bartanian is the founder and CEO of KEVANI, an outdoor advertising company specializing in full motion digital and premium static assets in California, New York & Maryland. 

In addition to KEVANI, he invests in startups and organizes community art events in Los Angeles.

experiences.title: Founder + CEO
experiences.description: KEVANI is an out of home media organization that promotes national and local brands through innovative outdoor advertising destinations across the US. Our inventory provides a unique opportunity for our brand and agency partners to captivate their audience.

experiences.title: Member, Innovations Commitee
experiences.description: The Innovations Committee identifies technologies that can be integrated into OOH media campaigns.

experiences.title: Executive Vice President, Sales & Business Development
experiences.description:
Firm transforms urban environments with digital monuments. Responsible for managing sales and business development operations, sales and marketing strategy development, client account management, deal negotiation, asset development, and establishing relationships with brand teams (as well as their agencies and media specialists).

► Presided over business development operation which experienced tremendous growth in a short period.

► Established in-house Digital Out-Of-Home media sales and content sponsorship programs resulting in a 300% increase in sales.

► Conducted media valuations for the largest digital OOH advertising developments in US, Mexico and U.A.E..

► Secured multiple high-profile tender wins.

experiences.title: Director, National Sales
experiences.description:
The world’s largest outdoor advertising company. Responsible for managing national sales operations, marketing strategy development, managing key client accounts, specialized sales presentation development, product management, establishing strategic partnerships, new product introduction, and client relations.

► Sales operation exceeded all company expectations and projections.

► Key clients included Apple, Warner Bros, Nest Labs, Blackrock, T-Mobile, Salesforce.com, Microsoft, GAP Brands, Google, Yahoo, ZICO and countless others.

experiences.title: Director, Sales & Marketing
experiences.description:
Company is a leading provider of mobile advertising services. Responsible for managing sales and marketing activities, staff training and development, managing key client accounts, marketing strategy development, client relations, and handling a wide variety of special projects. 

► Sales operation consistently exceeded companywide expectations, with the addition of more than 600 new clients.

► Developed business and operational models which greatly enhanced firm’s profitability.

experiences.title: Creative Director / Co-Founder
experiences.description:
Firm was a provider of interactive development services, as well as CD/DVD manufacturing. Responsible for managing daily company operations, sales management, marketing strategy development, overseeing product management activities, client relations, and other related assignments.

► Managed a sales operation which was integral in firm’s tremendous growth.

► Developed key relationships with clients, suppliers, manufactures, and overseas developers.

experiences.title: Assistant Director
experiences.description:
Responsible for assisting with daily hotel operations, staff training and supervision, preparing monthly executive presentations, conducting employee safety training, and resolving guest service matters. 

► Worked extensively within the loss prevention area, and reduced workers compensation claims.

► Supervised MOD and OSHA programs.

accomplishment_publications.name: Brands Are Dominating Billboards This Year: Here's How To Create Impactful OOH Advertising
DOOH expert cites neuroscience: 'Make it memorable'
Flipping the Switch with Kevin Bartanian of Kevani Inc.
accomplishment_publications.description: Kevin Bartanian sat down for a short Q&A session with DTLA Weekly publication. The talk was about the Outdoor Advertising landscape in DTLA and KEVANI's contributions to the area. He discusses the KEVANI approach to outdoor advertising that sets it apart from other billboard companies in Los Angeles and beyond.

accomplishment_publications.name: Tech Report 5.0: Digital Immersion
accomplishment_publications.description: “Our goal is to create an emotional connection between the viewer, the architecture, and, where appropriate, the brand,” explains Kevin Bartanian, He tells BD+C that on every project, his firm considers four things: the experience being created, how it will attract tenants or customers to the property, how it drives attention to other parts of the building, and how it “fosters community” by using local artists’ or students’ work.

accomplishment_publications.name: How digital signage can tap into visitors' emotions
accomplishment_publications.description: "Content designed to invoke emotion performs twice as well,"

accomplishment_publications.name: One Change for OOH Tech?
accomplishment_publications.description: I would have all creative for DOOH be adaptive and reactive. Cultural relevance has always been a driving factor in ad efficacy. Being very effective today in OOH requires advertisers to think about micro-moments and incorporate those moments in DOOH – as they happen, in real time. For example, when the lights went out at the 2013 Super Bowl, people took to social media and discussed the blackout, and when Oreo posted a funny tweet about the event, it generated a lot of buzz.

accomplishment_publications.name: Augmented and Virtual Reality Impacts to OOH
accomplishment_publications.description: "Augmented Reality (AR) is our view of reality as modified by a computer through sound, graphic, video, or otherwise. The end goal is simple – enhance one’s current perception of reality. With this definition in mind, it is easy to appreciate AR’s influence on OOH. AR extends viewer experiences beyond the confines of a display by allowing the viewer to use their personal smart devices to interact directly with ads. Brands can provide an incentive for viewers to participate (e.g., a game, a discount, a secret message). In this context, we must also consider the role of Virtual Reality (VR). While VR creates new platforms that closely resemble in-game advertising, VR is still a long way from impacting OOH. It’s not really a passive technology and requires expensive and invasive gear."

accomplishment_publications.name: Introducing CIRCA, Los Angeles
accomplishment_publications.description: “We are very pleased to add the CIRCA displays to StandardVision’s operations portfolio of integrated media solutions. We are especially excited to build upon our existing presence in DTLA, where we have been successfully operating the digital displays at nearby LA Live’s Courtyard by Marriott and Residence Inn. The CIRCA relationship demonstrates our continued success in leading the digital transformation in Out-of-Home advertising for one of the most powerful and influential cities in the world. We look forward to launching world class integrated advertising solutions at this one-of-a-kind development.” CIRCA’s Media Display Facts: - Over 18,000 Square feet of digital displays on Figueroa & 12th Street - Approximately 6,100 square feet of static display on Flower & 12th Street - State-of-the-art LED technology by Sansi North America (SNA) Displays

accomplishment_publications.name: Instagram Moments adding a new layer to apartment marketing
accomplishment_publications.description: "social media is a big factor in the surging interest in art of all forms in and around buildings. In fact, his company makes a point to ensure that all of its displays have a high enough resolution to present well when photographed by a smartphone. Though he doesn’t see digital art taking away market space from physical art in buildings, Bartanian said he sees it growing the pie. He noted that a digital display that’s constantly changing provides more incentive for people to visit and return to a space to see it—and, of course, keep their social feed fresh. Art as an attraction, be it for social uses or otherwise, has been on the rise for the past few years, Bartanian said, but where he’s seen the biggest growth is in the demand for quality." “Even if you have the best technology in the world, the best screen and largest screen, if you don’t have the best content possible that moves people and touches people, it’s not worth the investment and developers are realizing that,” he said. “People are getting more educated and smarter about it.”

accomplishment_publications.name: Public Art: The Unlikely Player Changing the Face of DOOH Advertising
accomplishment_publications.description: Defining public art according to Americans For The Arts: Public art is art in public spaces. It can take a wide range of forms, sizes, and scales, and it can be temporary or permanent. It is often site-specific—created in response to the place and community in which it resides—and it is free and accessible to everyone. In the world of DOOH advertising, public art is quickly becoming a surprising yet lucrative partner. While making a significant investment in digital, non-commercial content may seem counter-intuitive, this approach is garnering attention. And there is plenty of research to explain why.
